title: solanum indicum subsp. rohrii
intro: Solanum indicum subsp. rohrii, commonly known as Rohira or Rohini, is a plant species belonging to the Solanaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Rohira can be prepared into various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health concerns.
